mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and drive negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and consumer prices indicates demand-pull inflation that forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side contraction, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months with declining INDPRO during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal savings falling below $400 billion indicates depleted household balance sheets, reducing consumer spending capacity and depressing corporate earnings expectations for the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of months where RSAFS drops below $400 billion.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment alongside a falling unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPI growth during a monetary easing cycle signals stagflation risk, causing investors to rotate out of equities into real assets and generating negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months with CPIAUCSL above 235 during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts signal increased construction activity and durable goods demand, providing a positive short-term earnings catalyst that drives positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 36-month rolling window, or 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of high HOUST months.
